title: Travel and accommodation
description: A2 vocabulary and communication for travel: transport, accommodation, directions, and key prepositions. Includes the false friend *attendre* (to wait for, NOT to attend). Explained in English.

cards.front: le train, l'avion, le métro, le bus, la voiture
cards.notes: Core transport nouns. Note the articles: **le** train, **l'**avion (l' before a vowel), **le** métro, **le** bus, **la** voiture.

cards.front: en train, en avion, en voiture / à pied, à vélo
cards.back: by train, by plane, by car / on foot, by bike
cards.notes: Use **en** + motorised transport and **à** + non-motorised travel. **en** train/avion/voiture/bus/métro; **à** pied/vélo. There is no article after *en* or *à* here.

cards.front: à Paris, en France, au Canada, aux États-Unis
cards.back: in/to Paris, in/to France, in/to Canada, in/to the United States
cards.notes: **à** + city; **en** + feminine country (most ending in -e); **au** + masculine country; **aux** + plural country. France is feminine → *en France*; Canada is masculine → *au Canada*.

cards.front: prendre le train, un billet, un aller-retour, un aller simple
cards.back: to take the train, a ticket, a return ticket, a one-way ticket
cards.notes: *Prendre* is used for transport: *Je prends le train.* (I take/am taking the train.) *Un billet* = a ticket; *un aller-retour* = round-trip; *un aller simple* = one-way.

cards.front: l'hôtel, une chambre, réserver, une réservation, la clé, l'auberge de jeunesse
cards.back: the hotel, a room, to book/reserve, a reservation, the key, the youth hostel
cards.notes: *Réserver* is a regular -er verb: *Je voudrais réserver une chambre* (I would like to book a room). *La clé* is sometimes spelled *la clef* — both are correct.

cards.front: tournez à gauche, tournez à droite, tout droit, au coin, en face de, à côté de, jusqu'à
cards.back: turn left, turn right, straight ahead, at the corner, opposite/across from, next to, as far as/until
cards.notes: Use the imperative *tournez* (formal/plural) or *tourne* (informal) for directions. *Tout droit* = straight on (no turn). *Jusqu'à* combines with an article: *jusqu'au carrefour* (as far as the crossroads).

**False friend alert!** *Attendre* = **to wait for**, not 'to attend'. *J'attends le bus* = I am waiting for the bus. 'To attend' = **assister à**: *J'assiste au concert* (I am attending the concert).
cards.hint: attendre = to wait for; assister a = to attend.

cards.front: Je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux nuits.
cards.back: I would like to book a room for two nights.
cards.notes: *Je voudrais* (conditional of *vouloir*) is polite. *Pour deux nuits* = for two nights. You can also say *une chambre simple* (single room) or *une chambre double* (double room).

cards.front: Excusez-moi, pour aller à la gare, s'il vous plaît ?
cards.back: Excuse me, how do I get to the station, please?
cards.notes: Literally: 'Excuse me, to go to the station, please?' The *pour aller a...* structure is the standard way to ask for directions. *La gare* = the (train) station.

cards.front: Je dois prendre le métro jusqu'à Nation, puis tourner à gauche.
cards.back: I need to take the metro to Nation, then turn left.
cards.notes: *Devoir* + infinitive = must/have to. *Jusqu'a* + place = as far as / up to. *Puis* = then (sequence). This type of sentence chains a transport instruction with a direction.

cards.front: J'ai une réservation au nom de Dupont.
cards.back: I have a reservation in the name of Dupont.
cards.notes: *Au nom de* = in the name of. This is the standard phrase for checking in. You might also hear *Vous avez une réservation ?* (Do you have a reservation?) from the receptionist.

steps.title: Getting around: transport and prepositions
steps.body:
# Travel and accommodation

At A2 you need to handle real travel situations: buying tickets, checking in to a hotel, and asking for directions. This lesson covers the key vocabulary and two important grammar points.

## Transport vocabulary

*le train, l'avion, le métro, le bus, la voiture* — train, plane, metro, bus, car.

To say how you travel, use **en** or **à**:

| Preposition | Used with | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **en** | motorised transport | *en train, en avion, en voiture, en bus, en métro* |
| **à** | non-motorised | *à pied* (on foot), *à vélo* (by bike) |

## Cities, countries

| Location type | Preposition | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Any city | **à** | *à Paris, à Lyon* |
| Feminine country | **en** | *en France, en Espagne* |
| Masculine country | **au** | *au Canada, au Japon* |
| Plural country | **aux** | *aux États-Unis* |

> Most countries ending in **-e** are feminine → *en*. Canada, Mexique, Maroc, Portugal are masculine → *au*.

steps.title: Accommodation and directions
steps.body:
## Accommodation

Key words: *l'hôtel, une chambre, réserver, une réservation, la clé, l'auberge de jeunesse* (youth hostel).

Useful booking phrase:
> *Je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux nuits.* (I would like to book a room for two nights.)

## Direction phrases

| French | English |
|---|---|
| *tournez à gauche* | turn left |
| *tournez à droite* | turn right |
| *tout droit* | straight ahead |
| *au coin* | at the corner |
| *en face de* | opposite / across from |
| *à côté de* | next to |
| *jusqu'à* | as far as / until |

To ask for directions:
> *Excusez-moi, pour aller à la gare, s'il vous plaît ?* (Excuse me, how do I get to the station, please?)

steps.title: False friend: attendre vs. assister a
steps.body:
## False friend: *attendre*

**attendre** does NOT mean 'to attend'. It means **to wait for**.

| French | English |
|---|---|
| *J'attends le bus.* | I am waiting for the bus. |
| *Elle attend son ami.* | She is waiting for her friend. |

'To attend' = **assister a** + noun:

| French | English |
|---|---|
| *J'assiste au concert.* | I am attending the concert. |
| *Il assiste à la réunion.* | He is attending the meeting. |

> Note: *attendre* already contains the idea of 'for' — you do NOT say *attendre pour le bus*.

## Quick recap: buying a ticket

- *un billet* — a ticket
- *un aller simple* — a one-way ticket
- *un aller-retour* — a return ticket
- *Je voudrais un aller-retour pour Lyon.* — I would like a return ticket to Lyon.

steps.title: Match the transport to its preposition

exercise.prompt: Match each way of travelling to the correct French preposition (en or a).
Translate: *I travel to Paris by train.* Use *voyager* (to travel) + correct prepositions.
accept: Je voyage à Paris en train.
je voyage à Paris en train
Je voyage a Paris en train.
Je voyage à Paris en train
exercise.hint: City = a + city name; transport = en + vehicle.
distractors: Je voyage en Paris en train.
Je voyage à Paris au train.
Je voyage en Paris à train.

steps.title: Fill in the correct preposition before the country

exercise.prompt: Fill in the blank: France is feminine, so use the correct preposition.
exercise.hint: France is a feminine country ending in -e.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I am waiting for the bus.* (Watch out for the false friend!)
exercise.hint: attendre = to wait for. Do NOT use assister a here.

steps.title: Hotel scene: choose the correct phrase

exercise.prompt: A guest arrives at the reception desk. Which sentence would they use to check in?

images.label: J'ai une réservation au nom de Dupont.
Je voudrais un aller-retour, s'il vous plaît.
J'attends mon ami dans le couloir.

exercise.hint: Checking in involves confirming your reservation.
distractors: Je voudrais un aller-retour, s'il vous plait.
J'attends mon ami dans le couloir.

steps.title: Build: go straight ahead then turn left

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to say: *Go straight ahead, then turn left.*
exercise.hint: tout droit = straight ahead; tournez a gauche = turn left.

exercise.prompt: Match each French direction phrase to its English meaning.
Translate: *I would like to book a room for two nights.*
accept: Je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux nuits.
je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux nuits
Je voudrais reserver une chambre pour deux nuits.
Je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux nuits
exercise.hint: Je voudrais (I would like) + réserver (to book) + une chambre (a room) + pour deux nuits (for two nights).
distractors: Je veux attendre une chambre pour deux nuits.
Je voudrais réserver une clé pour deux nuits.
Je voudrais assister une chambre pour deux nuits.

steps.title: Fill in: false friend attendre in context

exercise.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct verb. She is waiting for her friend at the station.
exercise.sentence: Elle ___ son ami à la gare.
exercise.hint: attendre conjugated for elle (3rd person singular): j'attends, tu attends, il/elle attend.

steps.title: Build: I would like a return ticket to Lyon

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *I would like a return ticket to Lyon.*
